mechanism_narrative: Persistent decline in housing starts signals contraction in residential investment and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations deteriorate. This trend is always active as housing is a leading indicator.
failure_condition: If HOUST stabilizes or recovers and S&P 500 still declines in more than half of the 60-day windows, or Hit Ratio < 55% over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above its recent low signals labor market weakening, which reduces consumer income and spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the short term as markets price in recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by 0.2 percentage points or more within 3 months above its current level of 4.5%
failure_condition: If UNRATE rise is followed by positive S&P 500 returns in >50% of cases within 5 trading days over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high Fed funds rate and declining industrial production indicate that monetary tightening is successfully slowing the economy, raising recession risk and compressing equity valuation multiples, leading to negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 5.25% and INDPRO below its 6-month moving average
regime_hint: Monetary tightening with slowing real output
failure_condition: If Rank IC for the joint signal is below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or if the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When Fed funds are high and housing starts are low, the economy is in a late-cycle phase where further weakening in housing portends recession. This regime-conditional signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as the housing downturn spreads.
regime_hint: Monetary tightening cycle with housing market weakness
failure_condition: If the conditional signal has lower predictive power than the unconditional HOUST signal alone, or if it fails to achieve Hit Ratio > 55% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing activity and robust economic output, which supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month growth is negatively correlated with forward 20-day returns (Pearson r < -0.1) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in consumer price inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL triggers market expectations of tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and lowering equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent weeks.
failure_condition: If inflation spikes are followed by positive S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in both nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a broad-based economic slowdown, as labor income and consumer spending contract together. This joint signal reliably precedes negative S&P 500 returns as recession fears mount.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0 and RSAFS month-over-month change < 0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If simultaneous declines are followed by positive S&P 60-day returns in >40% of cases over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is robust (above 3% year-over-year) but industrial production unexpectedly declines, it signals that the expansion may be peaking. Markets interpret this as a warning of diminishing momentum, prompting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 3% and INDPRO month-over-month change < 0
regime_hint: Late expansion phase with robust GDP growth
failure_condition: If the signal fails to predict negative returns with accuracy >55% over any 3-year period, or if the condition occurs but subsequent returns are positive in >40% of instances.
